content.problemStatement:
For a set SSS of non-negative integers, MEX(S)MEX(S)MEX(S) is defined as the smallest non-negative integer which does not appear in SSS.

For a given permutation {P0,P1,P2,‚Ä¶,PN}\{P_0, P_1, P_2, \ldots, P_N\}{P0‚Äã,P1‚Äã,P2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,PN‚Äã} of integers 000 to NNN, we define the beauty of the permutation as ‚à£A‚àíB‚à£|A-B|‚à£A‚àíB‚à£, where:


A=‚àëi=0NMEX({P0,P1,P2,‚Ä¶,Pi})A = \sum_{i = 0}^N MEX(\{P_0, P_1, P_2, \ldots, P_i\})A=‚àëi=0N‚ÄãMEX({P0‚Äã,P1‚Äã,P2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,Pi‚Äã})
B=‚àëi=0NMEX({PN,PN‚àí1,‚Ä¶,PN‚àíi})B = \sum_{i = 0}^N MEX(\{P_N, P_{N-1}, \ldots, P_{N-i} \})B=‚àëi=0N‚ÄãMEX({PN‚Äã,PN‚àí1‚Äã,‚Ä¶,PN‚àíi‚Äã})
Find any permutation PPP with beauty KKK, or determine whether no such permutation exists.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT, the number of test cases.
The only line of each test case contains two integers NNN and KKK, where (N+1)(N+1)(N+1) is the length of the permutation and KKK is the desired beauty of the permutation.


Output Format
For each test case, if there is no permutation of integers from 000 to NNN with beauty KKK, output ‚àí1-1‚àí1.


Otherwise, output N+1N+1N+1 space-separated integers P0,P1,‚Ä¶,PNP_0, P_1, \ldots, P_NP0‚Äã,P1‚Äã,‚Ä¶,PN‚Äã (0‚â§Pi‚â§N)(0 \le P_i \le N)(0‚â§Pi‚Äã‚â§N), the needed permutation.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚ãÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: A valid permutation is P={0,3,1,2}P = \{0, 3, 1, 2\}P={0,3,1,2}. Here, A=1+1+2+4=8A = 1 + 1 + 2 + 4 = 8A=1+1+2+4=8, and B=0+0+0+4=4B = 0 + 0 + 0 + 4 = 4B=0+0+0+4=4, so ‚à£A‚àíB‚à£=4|A - B| = 4‚à£A‚àíB‚à£=4.
Test case 222: A valid permutation is P={1,2,0}P = \{1, 2, 0\}P={1,2,0}. Here, A=0+0+3=3A = 0 + 0 + 3 = 3A=0+0+3=3, and B=1+1+3=5B = 1 + 1 + 3 = 5B=1+1+3=5, so ‚à£A‚àíB‚à£=2|A - B| = 2‚à£A‚àíB‚à£=2.
Test case 333: There exists no valid permutation.
